title: X-83 TwinTail starfighter

{{Quote|Just saying the last ship I saw you on exploded…|Shado Vao, to Cade Skywalker, about the X-83|<ref name="L1">[[Legacy (2006) 1|''Legacy'' (2006) 1]]</ref>}}
The '''X-83 TwinTail starfighter''' was a model of [[X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing]] series [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] used before and during [[Darth Krayt]]'s rise to power in [[130 ABY]]. Manufactured by [[Incom Corporation/Legends|Incom Corporation]], the X-83 was a cutting-edge vessel and the primary starfighter of the [[New Jedi Order]] until its fall and decline following the [[Sith–Imperial War|Sith-Imperial War]]. [[Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire|Krayt's Empire]] banned the manufacture of the X-83, claiming it was too powerful for civilian use, but Incom continued producing the X-wings in secret, selling them to opponents of the Empire.

Drawing from the strengths of its lineage, the X-83 was balanced in durability and firepower, with top of the line weapons systems and strong [[Deflector shield/Legends|deflector shields]] and hull. It was a particularly maneuverable fighter as well, in [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]] or [[Vacuum/Legends|vacuum]]. The TwinTail incorporated its [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech droid]], an Incom standard feature, into its frame, giving the ship its own "personality" if not given routine [[Memory wipe/Legends|memory wipes]]. The fighter was primarily used in front-line [[Starfighter combat/Legends|combat]], where it was a match for most fighters, including the [[Predator-class fighter|''Predator''-class fighter]], on a one-to-one basis.

==Characteristics==
The X-83 was the successor to Incom's [[X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing]] line of [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]], and as of [[137 ABY]], the latest model of that series. It was built with many of the original X-wing's design principles in mind, including that model's versatility of role and its armament of four [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannons]], one at the tip of each wing, as well as the former models' iconic split-wing architecture that formed the craft's distinctive X-shaped profile.<ref name="SOTG" /><ref name="L0" /> Following up on the previous iteration of the X-wing, the [[R-28 starfighter]], the X-83 shared many of the parts used in its more commonplace predecessor.<ref name="LECG" />

The X-83 was one of the most cutting-edge starfighters of its time, made with superior components.<ref name="LECG" /> It was a balanced fighter, capable of assuming a number of roles due to its combination of firepower and durability. The fighter's shields were comparable to those of a [[Transport/Legends|transport]] or light [[Frigate/Legends|frigate]], and were able to absorb a good deal of fire before they failed. Its hull, too, was stronger than those of most [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttles]].<ref name="SOTG" /> It was vulnerable to intense weather, however, such as the electrical storms of [[Kiffex/Legends|Kiffex]].<ref name="L34">[[Legacy (2006) 34|''Legacy'' (2006) 34]]</ref>

[[File:LegacyDF.jpg|thumb|left|150px|An X-83 in action.]]
The fighter's strengths also lay in its weaponry, which were at the top of their field.<ref name="SOTG" /> With four laser cannons whose fire could be linked or fired in sequence,<ref name="L0" /> the X-83 could produce a high-energy blast that would destroy any target. Like previous models, these X-wings were also equipped with [[Proton torpedo/Legends|proton torpedoes]]. In one-on-one combat, the X-83 was easily superior to most contemporary fighters, and even when outnumbered was capable of inflicting heavy damage on the enemy.<ref name="SOTG" /> In [[Realspace/Legends|deep space]] or atmosphere, the X-83 was also unmatched in maneuverability,<ref name="L0½" /> due in part to a set of maneuvering vanes installed on each exhaust nacelle.<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|III|339}}</ref> It was this aspect that made it the favored fighter of the New Jedi Order.<ref name="L0½" />

The [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech droid]] of the X-83, a hallmark of Incom designs, was hardwired into the ship, turning it into more of an extension of the fighter's main computer.<ref name="LECG" /> Without frequent [[Memory wipe/Legends|memory wipes]] the craft could develop a personality of its own, with the droid exerting its own control over the ship's electronic systems.<ref name="SOTG" /> Incom recommended wiping its memory for this reason, as some of the personality flaws the droid could pick up—for example, an obstinate streak—could prove hazardous to the [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] if the droid refused to obey his or her commands.<ref name="LECG" /> This gained the ship a reputation for quirkiness. Some X-83 pilots, however, intentionally allowed the droids to develop personalities, believing that the performance and response of their ship improved when the astromech could anticipate their actions.<ref name="SOTG" />

X-83 pilots wore a [[Flight suit/Legends|flight suit]] that could be sealed against vacuum, and in an emergency they could jettison themselves from the cockpit.<ref name="L5">[[Legacy (2006) 5|''Legacy'' (2006) 5]]</ref> The X-83's cockpit, which had windows that extended to the sides and somewhat behind the pilot,<ref name="L1" /> was mounted further forward than the original T-65 starfighter.<ref name="CSWE" /> The cockpit was similarly lined with panels on all sides, and the pilot used two control yokes to operate the fighter.<ref name="L1" /> Unlike the typical four-engine configuration of its predecessors, the TwinTail boasted two long engines trailing further behind the fighter.<ref name="CSWE" />

==Role==
[[File:CadePilot-2006Legacy1.jpg|thumb|right|200px|[[Cade Skywalker]] piloting an X-83.]]
The X-83 earned a reputation for durability, and it was well suited to front-line service. X-83 [[Squadron/Legends|squadrons]] were often used to take the initial brunt of an attack, absorbing damage from enemy forces so the weaker fighters they were supporting could pass through unharmed.<ref name="SOTG" /> [[J-1 shuttle]]s also carried a single X-83 in their holds, which could be used as an escort.<ref name="L1" /> The X-83 could also be carried in medium-sized transports like the [[Helot-class medium space transport|''Helot''-class]].<ref name="L34" />

Before the fall of the Jedi Order, the X-83 was used frequently by that organization. With the rise of [[Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire]], TwinTails found their way into the arsenals of those groups and individuals who opposed him.<ref name="LECG" /> Jedi TwinTail squads, like [[Squad Three-Zero-One]], continued to fly in missions against the Empire.<ref name="L42">[[Legacy (2006) 42|''Legacy'' (2006) 42]]</ref>

==History==
[[File:J-1_shuttle_X-83_escort.jpg|thumb|left|150px|Two X-83s escort a [[J-1 shuttle|Jedi shuttle]].]]
The X-83 was developed by Incom as one of the long line of X-wing models, succeeding the R-28 starfighter. It attained common usage among the Jedi, but when [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Darth Krayt]] seized control of the [[Fel Empire|Galactic Empire]] in [[130 ABY]] and exterminated the Jedi, the Empire deemed the TwinTail too powerful for civilian use; in an effort to limit the firepower available to individual citizens and non-Imperial organizations, the Empire ordered that Incom halt production of it. Incom, however, resisted in secret. A number of the X-83's manufacturing plants were only closed in appearance, and after being reopened clandestinely they continued producing the starfighters. Other factories kept open to produce the commonly used R-28 took advantage of the fact that the two fighters shared many of the same parts, turning out the occasional X-83 when Imperial inspectors were looking elsewhere. The Empire was unable to find any evidence of this secret manufacturing, though rumors about it circulated, and many of the X-wings produced after the rise of Krayt were sold through the black market and ended up in the hands of those who opposed the Empire.<ref name="LECG" />

Even during and after Krayt's takeover, the Jedi retained usage of a number of X-83s. When a J-1 shuttle carrying a number of [[Padawan/Legends|Padawans]] escaped the [[massacre at Ossus]] on the day of Krayt's coup, one of its passengers, [[Cade Skywalker]], took the shuttle's X-83 to fight off pursuing Imperial [[Predator-class fighter|''Predator''-class starfighters]]. Skywalker was able to hold off the enemy craft as the shuttle prepared to leap to [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]], and in the end sacrificed his X-wing, jettisoning himself from the fighter at the last moment.<ref name="L5" /><ref name="L1" /> Years later, Skywalker participated in an [[Mission to Had Abbadon|attack]] on an Imperial base on [[Had Abbadon]], in which fellow Jedi [[Shado Vao]] piloted an X-83 of his own alongside the ''Predators'' of his [[Imperial Knight]] allies.<ref name="L29">[[Legacy (2006) 29|''Legacy'' (2006) 29]]</ref>

Shortly after the Had Abaddon mission, a task force of Jedi in X-83s—Squad Three-Zero-One—rendezvoused with the fleet of rogue [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances|Galactic Alliance]] admiral [[Gar Stazi]] to discuss an alliance. When the fleet's location was betrayed to the Empire by [[Tealart|one of Stazi's crew]], the TwinTails joined the battle against incoming Imperial forces until the rest of the fleet could make its escape.<ref name="L42" />

==Behind the scenes==
The X-83 was designed by [[Jan Duursema]] for the ''[[Star Wars: Legacy]]'' comic series.<ref name="JCF">{{JCFCite|url=posts/27015823|board=Literature|thread=Galactic Alliance Remnant discussion thread (Possible Legacy Spoilers)|user=Madine|date=2007-06-28|time=4:17 PM|archiveurl=https://archive.is/PXkac}}</ref>
